_description: Complete catalog of all 33 ChartFanatics strategies with detailed rules. Organized by automation applicability tier. Used as reference by Claude decision layer and future strategy modules.

tier_1_ict_native._description: Directly map to existing ICT pipeline. No new analysis modules needed — just new combination rules.

rules: Identify clear market structure break (MSB) on HTF establishing trend
Mark range from swing low to swing high (uptrend) or high to low (downtrend)
Identify 50% midpoint dividing premium/discount zones
Mark POI: Order Block or Breaker Block where pullbacks occur
Wait for engineered liquidity (swing low/high formation) on LTF inside POI
Identify liquidity sweep (stop run) on LTF
Enter at breaker block after MSB on LTF
entry.long: Buy higher lows in discount zones
entry.short: Sell lower highs in premium zones

exit.stop: Below swept lows (longs) / above swept highs (shorts)
exit.target: Next HTF external liquidity (previous swing high/low)

rules: All three timeframes (D1, H4, H1) align around same 50% level
Manipulation move occurs around 10:00 AM ET
SMT divergence appears (e.g. NQ new high but ES doesn't, or BTC new high but ETH doesn't)
Price rejects inversion zone (prior imbalance acting as S/R)
entry.trigger: Sell stop below inversion zone (shorts) or buy stop above (longs)

exit.be_rule: Move to breakeven once hourly candle confirms direction

strategies.name: PO3, OTE + ADR (Market Maker Model)

rules: Price must open near a PD Array (PDH/PDL, FVG, OB, weekly levels, breaker/mitigation blocks)
Establish daily bias beforehand (bullish or bearish ONLY — never trade both ways)
Confirm via Smart Money Reversal (SMR): high -> low -> higher high -> lower low on 15M/30M
Displacement candle must close with body (not wick) past key level
Draw Fibonacci from swing high to low
Enter ONLY at OTE retracement: 0.50, 0.62, 0.705, or 0.79 level
Reject entries above 50% retracement (insufficient R:R)
entry.trigger: Limit order at 0.62 or 0.705 Fibonacci level preferred

exit.stop: 1.0 Fibonacci level (swing extreme) or 0.90 for tighter risk
exit.target: 0.0 Fibonacci level, then trail if momentum continues
exit.be_rule: Move to breakeven when price closes past 0.20 Fibonacci level

strategies.time_filter: London Open (2-5am ET) or NY Open (7-10am ET)

rules: Identify Accumulation phase: price moves sideways, builds liquidity, traps traders in chop
Wait for Manipulation: sharp move sweeping liquidity above or below range
Require Displacement: strong break of swing high/low leaving FVG behind
Enter on retrace into the valid FVG
Never chase — if no retracement, skip the trade
entry.trigger: Entry on retrace into FVG left by displacement candle

exit.target: Partial at swing levels or next FVG, trail remainder
exit.management: Trail stops to protect profits when price confirms move

rules: Identify a high/low that price respected and moved away from (creates resting liquidity)
Wait for price to return and trade through that level (liquidity taken)
Confirm with internal structure or false reaction (trap confirmed)
Execute AFTER liquidity is taken, never before
Buy below lows, sell above highs

exit.stop: Below swept low (longs) / above swept high (shorts)
exit.target: Opposing liquidity at next major highs/lows
exit.partials: Only at actual liquidity zones, not arbitrary R-multiples

rules: Establish daily bias from daily chart (recent highs, lows, inefficiencies)
Mark session liquidity: PDH/PDL, Asian High/Low, London High/Low
Wait for key session level to be taken out during NY session (liquidity raid)
Confirm on LTF (15M/5M) via: FVG, MSS, Turtle Soup, or Breaker Block
Enter between 9:30-11:30 AM ET

exit.stop: Just above swept high (shorts) / below swept low (longs)
exit.target: Opposite session liquidity zones, FVGs, or equal highs/lows

strategies.name: Unique High RR / Trident Pattern

rules: 3-candle FVG must form on 30M chart between 2:30-4:00 AM ET
Mark FVG 50% midpoint (reaction zone)
Small doji candle must form next, wicking into FVG 50% zone
Confirmation candle must close below doji high (above = invalid)
EMA stack (5/9/13/21) must be clearly stacked in trade direction
200 EMA determines bias: above = longs only, below = shorts only
entry.trigger: On confirmation candle close or limit at FVG 50%

exit.stop: Below the FVG candle low (standard); no hard stop for Gold
exit.target: Ride trend via daily chart structure; exit when EMAs reverse

strategies.frequency: handful of times per year per pair

tier_2_price_action._description: Need minor analysis additions (trendline detection, key level scoring).

strategies.name: Break & Retest

rules: Mark major prior level (PDH/PDL, premarket high/low, supply/demand zone)
Wait for clean break with momentum (do NOT enter during break)
Wait for pullback to retest the zone (battle zone)
Confirmation candle: wicks into level and closes in trade direction

exit.stop: Beyond invalidation point (below level for longs, above for shorts)
exit.target: TP1 at prior high/low, then trail

strategies.ntz: Mark no-trade zone between PDH and premarket low — avoid trading inside

strategies.name: Trendline Break Pocket

rules: Price reaches HTF key level (support/resistance zone, identified by frequency + proximity)
Properly defined trendline broken
Last swing in old direction broken (momentum confirmation)
Entry Type 1 (primary): Pullback to 21 EMA
Entry Type 2: Consolidation breakout (min 2 highs and 2 lows)

exit.stop: Half the distance to target (2R structure)
exit.requirement: No major level blocking the 2R target path (clean-air rule)

strategies.overshoot_rule: One overshoot acceptable; two voids the setup

strategies.name: Trendline Strategy

rules: 2-3 clear touchpoints
1+ week between first touch and entry
Enter at trendline test
Exactly 2 touchpoints before break
1+ week of data
Break must be close to safety line
3+ touchpoints
1+ week of data
Enter on break with clear safety line

exit.stop: Safety line (opposing trendline). Close if price closes through safety line.
exit.management: Trail stop along trendline as new swing points form

indicators: none — pure price action with manual trendlines

strategies.name: Measured Move Trend Strategy

rules: Identify clear trend with consistent higher highs/lows or lower highs/lows
Draw trendline across pullback highs (shorts) or pullback lows (longs)
Identify candle with most extreme price within structure
Measure vertical distance from extreme candle to trendline
Wait for pullback to reject trendline
Enter as price moves back toward trend direction after rejection

exit.stop: Above trendline or recent swing high (shorts) / below trendline or swing low (longs)
exit.target: Measured move projection (same distance from extreme to trendline)

strategies.pattern_degradation: 1st and 2nd patterns strongest; 4th-5th patterns exhaust — reduce or avoid

tier_3_order_flow_volume._description: Need volume profile data or order flow feeds.

entry.framework: Context (market structure) + Location (historical reaction level) + Confirmation (order flow)
setups: Absorption Short: aggressive buyers fail at resistance, passive sellers absorb
Stop Run + Reclaim Long: flush below support triggering stops, then reclaim

rules: Price touches volume profile edge (HVA to LVA transition)
Alignment with ONH, ONL, PDH, or PDL
Signal candle with wick rejecting area and closing in trade direction
Wait for candle close — no front-running
Volatility variant: limit order at 50-80% wick retrace

exit.stop: Beyond signal candle wick or HVA edge
exit.target: Edge-to-edge (next high-volume shelf). POC retest common.

rules: Mark clear supply/demand/S&R level with prior consolidation then impulsive move away
Use volume-by-price to find LVN zones (price moved quickly, little volume)
Wait for price return to LVN zone
Verify with order flow (heatmap, footprint, delta showing defenders)

exit.target: Next HVA, LVN, or S/R level

strategies.name: Auction Market Theory

setups.name: Failed Auction Below (Reversal Long)
setups.trigger: Aggressive sellers fail, buyers absorb

setups.name: Failed Auction Above (Reversal Short)
setups.trigger: Aggressive buyers fail, sellers absorb

setups.name: Breakout with Acceptance (Continuation)
setups.trigger: Price holds at new level, participation continues

strategies.core_filter: Is the aggressive side being accepted or absorbed?

models.name: Trend Continuation
rules: Market out of balance with displacement
Identify impulse leg
Apply VP to locate LVNs
Enter when price returns to LVN WITH aggressive order flow (footprint imbalance)

models.name: Mean Reversion
rules: Market in balance/consolidation
Price pushes out then FAILS and reclaims
Wait for pullback into reclaim leg LVN
Enter on pullback with order flow confirmation

exit.stop: Just beyond aggressive print with 1-2 tick buffer

rules: Daily chart shows clear directional trend
5-min chart mirrors daily trend
Price aligns with 21/50 EMA on both timeframes
Identify congestion zones on 5-min (prior consolidation areas)
Wait for price to return to congestion zone
Enter when price rejects zone in direction of trend (rejection candle)

entry.long: Aggressive buyers absorb sell orders at DNA zone (tape confirms repeated buyer aggression)
entry.short: Aggressive sellers absorb buy orders at DNA zone (sellers repeatedly hit bids)

exit.partial: At first strong reaction or intraday pivot
exit.full_exit: When opposing side dominance confirmed on tape

tier_4_momentum_reversion._description: Complementary signals — mean reversion, momentum exhaustion, catalyst-driven.

conditions: Large rapid price move (minutes/hours, not months)
entry.long_trigger: Break above prior bar high after downtrend structure break
entry.short_trigger: Break below prior bar low after uptrend structure break
entry.principle: Wait for confirmation that move has turned — don't predict reversals

exit.stop: Below capitulation low (longs) / above parabolic high (shorts)
exit.trail: Below prior bar lows (longs) / above prior bar highs (shorts)

strategies.name: Episodic Pivot Strategy

setups.name: Classical Growth EP
setups.trigger: Triple-digit sales growth + large gap with heavy volume

setups.name: Turnaround EP
setups.trigger: Credible shift from declining to improving earnings

setups.name: Story/Thematic EP
setups.trigger: Powerful narrative + extreme volume

setups.name: EP 9 Million
setups.trigger: Volume >9M shares with clear trend

setups.name: Delayed Reaction EP
setups.trigger: Secondary move after consolidation post-catalyst

setups.name: Sugar Babies
setups.trigger: Volume breakouts on habitual runners with 40-50% swings

exit.stop: Below Day 1 opening-range low or nearest swing low
exit.trail: Below daily swing lows as trend develops

strategies.notes: Stock-specific (catalyst/earnings driven). Crypto equivalent = exchange listing pumps, major protocol upgrades.

strategies.name: Parabolic Short Strategy

conditions: Strong prior move from recent base (small-caps ~200%, mid-caps ~100%, large-caps ~50%)
Acceleration structure: expanding candles, steep slope, possible gaps
Exhaustion day: price stops making higher highs, forms lower highs/lows, loses VWAP
entries_ranked: Earliest: Break of upward structure with lower highs/lows (higher risk)
Stronger: Break structure AND lose VWAP simultaneously
Best: Price attempts VWAP reclaim and FAILS

exit.stop: High of the day or most recent lower high
exit.management: Partial profits once favorable; close before end of day — no overnight holds
exit.add: Risk-free add at VWAP retest once stop is at break-even

strategies.useful_concepts: VWAP reclaim-fail pattern applicable to any liquid market. Exhaustion day detection (lower highs forming) programmable.

strategies.name: First Red Day (Kyle Williams)

rules: Minimum 2-3 consecutive green days preceding
80-100%+ price extension from start of run
Expanding daily volume throughout advance
Expanding daily range showing acceleration
Aggressive: short before breaking prior day's close, stop at ATH
Standard: wait for crack below prior day's close, add on failed bounces
Conservative: wait for breakdown, short on failed lower high

exit.target: Minimum -10% daily decline for small caps; VWAP as magnet
exit.management: Cover into weakness; scale out; trail using 15-min chart high

strategies.risk: Max 3-5 attempts per stock; scale by grade (A+/A/B/C)
strategies.useful_concepts: Consecutive green days + extension % + volume expansion are fully quantifiable. Prior day's close break is clean trigger.

strategies.name: First Red Day (Alex Temiz variant)

rules: 3+ consecutive strong green days with parabolic move
Each day ideally stronger than previous
Stock must show extension and hype
Draw line at previous day's close
Starter position when stock breaks below low of day
Main position on break below previous day's close

variants.gap_up: Gaps up after run, pushes higher at open, then fades
variants.gap_down: Gaps down; wait for bounce toward PDC that fails

exit.invalidation: If price reclaims PDC and holds above it: cut immediately
exit.management: Lock in gains by covering portions into weakness

strategies.useful_concepts: Very clean rule-based system. PDC as invalidation is one of the cleanest stop rules.

tier_5_systematic_meta._description: Systematic/algorithmic frameworks, psychological meta-strategies, and educational content.

strategies.name: Algorithmic Strategy

framework.position_sizing: 5-25% of account per strategy, avg loss ~0.5%

strategies.key_principle: The more rules added, the more likely the system fits past data and fails live

strategies.name: Futures Trading Strategy

regimes.name: Consolidation
regimes.action: Trade both directions at range edges; stay out of middle

regimes.name: Expansion
regimes.action: ONE direction only; enter after breakout confirmation

regimes.name: Mean Reversion
regimes.action: Wait for daily close below 30% Fib line of BB peak-to-peak; target 50% level

exit.expansion: Scale out at weekly highs/lows, prior BB peaks
exit.mean_reversion: Exit at 50% level, then hands-in-pocket zone

strategies.useful_concepts: BB regime classification is fully programmable. Three-state machine (consolidation/expansion/mean reversion) maps cleanly to algorithmic logic.

strategies.name: VIX Futures Strategy

setups.name: ES breaks PDL + VIX confirms
setups.trigger: ES below PDL while VIX reaches/exceeds PDH = real institutional hedging; short ES

setups.name: VIX Double Bottom
setups.trigger: VIX double bottom at contract lows = bounce pressure on ES

setups.name: VIX Moves First
setups.trigger: VIX reaches PDH BEFORE ES breaks PDL = early warning

setups.name: Relative Strength Divergence
setups.trigger: ES breaks PDL but NQ holds + VIX weak = avoid short

entry.one_pct_rule: ES up 1%+ AND VIX up 1%+ = fragile strength. ES down 1%+ AND VIX down 1%+ = weak selling.

strategies.useful_concepts: VIX/ES correlation fully quantifiable. 1% divergence rule is clean filter. For crypto: maps to funding rate / open interest divergence.

strategies.name: Real Simple Strategy

setups.name: Episodic Pivot
setups.trigger: Large gap up on earnings + volume multiples above avg; enter on 5-min ORB

setups.name: High Volume Close
setups.trigger: Gap day + above-avg volume; enter on break through prior HVC

setups.name: Flat Base Breakout
setups.trigger: Horizontal consolidation, tight 10/20 MAs, shrinking ranges; enter above resistance

setups.name: Undercut & Rally
setups.trigger: Quick undercut of prior low then reversal; enter on reclaim

setups.name: MA Undercut & Rally
setups.trigger: Dip below 10/20/50 MA then reclaim with volume

setups.name: High Tight Flag
setups.trigger: 50-100% advance + tight 2-5 week flag + contracting volume; enter on break

exit.hard_exit: Close below key level on high volume or bearish reversal candle

strategies.useful_concepts: All 6 setups have quantifiable triggers. Flat Base Breakout and Undercut & Rally are universal patterns.

strategies.name: Universal Strategy

three_step_framework: 1. Liquidity Catalyst: sweep of equal highs/lows, major S/R break, trendline break, MSS, or sharp rejection
2. Displacement: decisive move confirming the catalyst mattered
3. Retracement: enter ONLY on pullback to broken S/R, FVG, prior swing, trendline retest, or 21 EMA

exit.stop: Beyond price level that invalidates the narrative (structure-based, not arbitrary)
exit.target: Liquidity pools, prior highs/lows, imbalance fills, trapped trader zones

strategies.useful_concepts: Three-step framework (catalyst -> displacement -> retracement) is extremely automation-friendly. Directly applicable to all markets in our pipeline.

rules: Identify major S/R from past highs/lows on daily/weekly charts
Focus on psychological round numbers (5000, 6000 SPX)
Price must reach key level concurrent with major catalyst (FOMC, CPI, earnings)
Confirm via strong bounce/rejection, multi-day hold, or sentiment shift

exit.sizing: Risk only what you're comfortable losing completely (size for zero)

strategies.name: Full Psychology Masterclass

key_concepts.yerkes_dodson: Optimal performance at moderate emotional arousal
key_concepts.progressive_shutdown: At 60% emotion retain awareness; at 80%+ cannot stop poor decisions
key_concepts.circuit_breakers: 2 consecutive losses = stop; emotional carryover = no trade

strategies.useful_concepts: Validates automation over discretion. Maps to: max consecutive loss limits, daily DD circuit breakers, session-based trading windows.

strategies.name: Shorting Strategy

setups.name: Gap-Up Short with Fade
setups.trigger: Sharp pre-market gap on low volume; below open by 10AM; short into bounces

setups.name: Backside Parabolic Short
setups.trigger: Large move completed; upper wicks, failed pushes; volume declining; short bounces during fade

setups.name: Multi-Halt Exhaustion
setups.trigger: 4-5 up-halts on low-float stock; enter after halt exhaustion; conservative sizing

strategies.time_filter: 4:00 AM - 10:00 AM primary; reduced edge after 12:00 PM
strategies.10am_rule: Below open = hold; above open = reduce risk or exit
strategies.recycling_framework: Partial profits into drops, re-enter on failed bounces into resistance
strategies.useful_concepts: Recycling framework (partial profit + re-entry on bounce failure) useful for any market.
